ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Text-based PDF. Clear and legible. Minor typos present in certificate: 'accoring' instead of 'according', 'packege' instead of 'package', 'Inovice' instead of 'Invoice', 'Wareparts' instead of 'Wearparts'. Consignee name appears truncated: 'Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A' (missing '.S' or 'A.Ş.').

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Text-based PDF. Clear and legible. 2 pages. Invoice no. 1418, dated 2026-02-11. Issued by NOx-SOL AB.

document_pairing.method: Certificate references invoice no. 1418, dated 2026-02-11. Invoice file contains Commercial Invoice no. 1418 dated 2026-02-11. Consignor NOx-SOL AB matches invoice issuer NOx-SOL AB.

criteria_met: Certificate invoice reference '1418' matches invoice number '1418'.
Certificate consignor 'NOx-SOL AB' matches invoice issuer 'NOx-SOL AB'.
Certificate consignee 'Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A' matches invoice buyer 'Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S'.

locations: Invoice page 1: header/branding 'NOx-SOL' and footer 'NOx-SOL AB'
Invoice page 1: bottom signature line 'NOx-SOL AB'
Invoice page 2: footer address block 'NOx-SOL AB, Aklejagatan 9, 434 46 Kungsbacka, Sweden'

candidates.normalized_text: ft1517 flowmeter water duk-12g4h-c3t00 (country of origin: germany)

candidates.raw_text: The exporter of the products covered by this document declares that except where otherwise clearly indicated, these products are of European Community preferential origin.

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name 'NOx-SOL AB' in all invoice pages.
comparison_steps.result: FOUND. 'NOx-SOL AB' appears in invoice page 1 header/branding, page 1 signature line, and page 2 footer address block. Name is an exact match.
Invoice page 2 footer explicitly states 'Sweden' in address block. Additionally VAT reg. No. 'SE559217701701' confirms SE (Sweden) prefix. MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found explicitly in invoice
Land verification via explicit address and VAT prefix

consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor is 'NOx-SOL AB' at 'Aklejagatan 9, 43446, KUNGSBACKA, Sweden'. Invoice issuer is 'NOx-SOL AB' with address 'Aklejagatan 9, 434 46 Kungsbacka, Sweden' (page 2 footer). The company name matches exactly after normalization. The country Sweden is confirmed via the explicit address and VAT prefix 'SE'. Minor formatting difference in postal code (43446 vs 434 46) is a permitted normalization.
consignor.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'NOx-SOL AB' kan identifieras exakt i fakturans sidhuvud, signaturrad och sidfot. Adress och land (Sweden) överensstämmer. MATCH enligt 4.1.0 och 4.1.2.1.

consignee.certificate_value: Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A, Barbaros Mah. Kardelen Sok. Palladium Tower No. 2/1 K:37-38, 347 46 Atasehir - Istanbul, Turkey
consignee.invoice_value: Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S, Barbaros Mah. Kardelen Sok. Palladium Tower No:2/1 K:37-38, 34746 Ataşehir-İstanbul, Turkey

comparison_steps.description: Invoice has a right-side address block for 'Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S' which serves as the buyer/bill-to party. No explicit 'Invoice to' or 'Bill to' label but this is the primary customer address block.
comparison_steps.result: Primary buyer identified as 'Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S'.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states 'Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A' – this appears to be truncated. The full name in invoice is 'Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S'. Certificate text 'Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A' is an exact prefix of the invoice name 'Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S' (after normalization of ç/c). The truncation drops '.S' which is clearly a layout/field limitation. No other party with similar name exists in the invoice.
comparison_steps.result: Layout truncation conditions met: (1) Certificate text is exact prefix of invoice name, (2) truncation at end of field, (3) no other similar party, (4) land matches (Turkey). MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states Turkey. Invoice buyer address states Turkey.

rules_applied.description: Layout truncation – certificate name is exact prefix of invoice name, '.S' truncated due to field limitation

consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee is 'Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A'. Invoice buyer is 'Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S'. The certificate name appears truncated – it ends with 'A' while the full name in the invoice is 'A.S' (Anonim Şirketi). The certificate text is an exact prefix of the invoice name after normalization. This is a clear layout/field truncation (rule 4.2.2.1.1). The minor difference in 'ç' vs 'c' (Akçansa vs Akcansa) is a character encoding variation, not a substantive difference. Land Turkey matches. Address details are consistent (Barbaros Mah. Kardelen Sok. Palladium Tower, Atasehir-Istanbul).

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The missing '.S' could indicate a different legal entity
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: The certificate text is an exact prefix of the invoice name. The truncation at 'A' (dropping '.S') is clearly due to field limitation in the certificate. No other entity with a similar name exists. Rule 4.2.2.1.1 applies.

consignee.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A' är en exakt inledande del (prefix) av fakturans köparnamn 'Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S'. Avkortningen ('.S' saknas) beror uppenbart på fältbegränsning i certifikatet. Land (Turkey) överensstämmer. MATCH enligt 4.2.2.1.1.

goods_description.certificate_value: Spare and Wareparts for SNCR-Equipment accoring to Commercial Invoice no. 1418
goods_description.invoice_value: FT1517 Flowmeter Water DUK-12G4H-C3T00; IP0601 I/P-converter 500-X Model: 500-ACD Contr.air 4-20mA

comparison_steps.description: Certificate uses a general goods description ('Spare and Wareparts for SNCR-Equipment') with an explicit reference to invoice no. 1418. Check if conditions for Prioritet 3 are met.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate states 'accoring to Commercial Invoice no. 1418' – this is an explicit invoice reference. Certificate also states in box 5: 'Inovice no. 1418 dat. 2026-02-11'. Invoice number 1418 matches. Invoice date 2026-02-11 matches. The phrase 'accoring to Commercial Invoice' is a clear (misspelled) form of 'according to Commercial Invoice'. The invoice contains items that are spare parts/components (Flowmeter Water, I/P-converter) which are consistent with 'Spare and Wareparts for SNCR-Equipment'. Conditions for 4.3.5 are met.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate does not list specific article numbers in box 6. No article numbers to verify via Prioritet 1.
comparison_steps.result: No article numbers in certificate. Prioritet 1 not applicable.

rules_applied.description: Generell varubeskrivning med fakturareferens – invoice no. 1418 and date 2026-02-11 match. Explicit reference 'accoring to Commercial Invoice no. 1418'. Goods description is consistent with invoice content.

goods_description.debug_explanation: Certificate uses a general goods description: 'Spare and Wareparts for SNCR-Equipment accoring to Commercial Invoice no. 1418'. This is a Prioritet 3 case (generell varubeskrivning med fakturareferens). The certificate explicitly references invoice no. 1418 with date 2026-02-11 in the remarks field (box 5) and again in box 6 with 'accoring to Commercial Invoice no. 1418'. The invoice file is Commercial Invoice no. 1418 dated 2026-02-11 – both reference and date match exactly. The invoice contains spare parts (Flowmeter Water, I/P-converter) which are consistent with 'Spare and Wareparts for SNCR-Equipment'. No article numbers are listed in the certificate, so Prioritet 1 is not applicable. According to rule 4.3.5, when a general description with matching invoice reference and date is present and the invoice content is consistent with the description, the goods description is considered verified.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The misspelling 'Wareparts' instead of 'Wearparts' or 'Spare parts' could indicate a discrepancy
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: The misspelling is in the certificate itself and does not affect the invoice reference verification. The phrase clearly intends 'spare and wear parts' or 'spare parts'. Under rule 4.3.5, the verification relies on the invoice reference, not on textual matching of the goods description.

goods_description.motivation: Certifikatet använder en generell varubeskrivning ('Spare and Wareparts for SNCR-Equipment') med uttrycklig hänvisning till faktura nr 1418 daterad 2026-02-11. Fakturanummer och datum överensstämmer exakt. Fakturans innehåll (reservdelar/komponenter för SNCR-utrustning) är förenligt med certifikatets beskrivning. MATCH enligt 4.3.5.

comparison_steps.description: Box 7 in certificate contains: '46*33*22cm'. This is a dimension specification, not a weight or numeric quantity with a standard quantity unit.
comparison_steps.result: Box 7 contains dimensions only. Multiple other quantity indications exist in box 6: 'One packege' (package quantity), 'Gross: 8kg', 'Net:7kg'.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states 'One packege' in box 6 – this is a shipment/package quantity ('1 package'). Per rule 4.4.3.2, this type of quantity is considered verified when the certificate contains a verifiable invoice reference per 4.3.5.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate references invoice 1418 with date 2026-02-11 which has been verified in goods description checkpoint. Shipment quantity '1 package' is accepted per 4.4.3.2.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states 'Gross: 8kg'. Invoice page 2 states 'Weight and Dimensions: 8kg'. Numeric value 8 matches. Unit kg matches. Weight category: certificate says 'Gross', invoice says 'Weight' without explicit GW/NW specification.
comparison_steps.result: Per 4.4.2.2: certificate states Gross: 8kg. Invoice states '8kg' without category. Certificate also states Net:7kg. Since certificate has BOTH GW (8kg) and NW (7kg), and invoice states '8kg' without category, we need to assess: the invoice does not explicitly label the weight as GW or NW. However, per 4.4.3.1, we need to verify MINST EN quantity. The shipment quantity (1 package) is already verified. The numeric value 8kg can be identified in the invoice. Per 4.4.2.2 point 3: certificate has multiple weight values, invoice has one value without category → would normally be MANUAL_REVIEW. However, per 4.4.3.1 and the critical delineation 4.4.6.1 vs 4.4.3.1: since the shipment quantity (1 package) has already been verified as MATCH, the quantity checkpoint has met its verification requirement. The 8kg value can also be identified in the invoice (8kg matches 8kg numerically). No explicit contradiction exists.

rules_applied.description: Shipment quantity '1 package' verified via invoice reference per 4.3.5
At least one quantity verified (shipment quantity). Additional weight 8kg can be identified in invoice.

quantity.debug_explanation: Certificate contains multiple quantity indications: (1) 'One packege' – shipment quantity in box 6, (2) 'Gross: 8kg' in box 6, (3) 'Net:7kg' in box 6, (4) '46*33*22cm' in box 7 (dimensions). Box 7 contains only dimensions, not a weight or numeric quantity with standard unit. The primary verifiable quantities are the shipment quantity and weights in box 6. Per rule 4.4.3.2, 'One packege' (=1 package) is a valid shipment quantity that is considered verified when the certificate contains a verifiable invoice reference (which it does per 4.3.5). This satisfies the 'at least one quantity verified' requirement of 4.4.3.1. Additionally, the gross weight 8kg can be identified in the invoice ('Weight and Dimensions: 8kg'). The net weight 7kg does not appear separately in the invoice, but per 4.4.3.1, this does not cause MISMATCH since the invoice does not explicitly contradict the NW value – the invoice simply doesn't state NW separately. Per the critical delineation rule (4.4.6.1 vs 4.4.3.1), since at least one quantity (shipment quantity) is verified as MATCH, the checkpoint requirement is met.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MANUAL_REVIEW because invoice weight '8kg' lacks GW/NW category while certificate has both GW and NW
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per 4.4.3.1 and the critical delineation 4.4.6.1 vs 4.4.3.1: the shipment quantity (1 package) has already been verified as MATCH via 4.4.3.2. This satisfies the 'at least one quantity verified' requirement. The weight verification is supplementary. The invoice does not explicitly CONTRADICT the GW value – it states '8kg' which matches the GW value. No NW value in the invoice contradicts the certificate's NW. Therefore MATCH overall for this checkpoint.

quantity.motivation: Certifikatet anger 'One packege' (1 paket) som försändelsekvantitet, verifierad via fakturareferens. Dessutom överensstämmer bruttovikten 8 kg med fakturans viktangivelse '8kg'. Minst en kvantitetsuppgift är verifierad. MATCH enligt 4.4.3.2 och 4.4.3.1.

country_of_origin.invoice_value: Country of Origin: Germany (FT1517); Country of Origin: USA (IP0601)

comparison_steps.description: Invoice contains per-article origin fields: 'Country of Origin: Germany' for FT1517, 'Country of Origin: USA' for IP0601.
comparison_steps.result: Two origin countries identified: Germany, USA.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states 'Germany / USA'. Invoice states 'Germany' and 'USA' per article. Both countries from certificate are explicitly present in invoice.

Certificate has multiple origins (Germany / USA) but the goods description is general ('Spare and Wareparts for SNCR-Equipment') with reference to invoice. Per 4.3.7, when the certificate contains an explicit reference to the invoice, the coupling requirement is satisfied via the invoice.
comparison_steps.result: Coupling requirement satisfied via invoice reference. Invoice clearly assigns Germany to FT1517 and USA to IP0601.

comparison_steps.step: 4.5.4.1 EU check – are there non-EU origins in invoice not covered by certificate?
comparison_steps.description: Invoice also contains a general EU declaration ('products are of European Community preferential origin'). Certificate states 'Germany / USA' – Germany is an EU member, USA is not. USA is explicitly stated in both certificate and invoice. The EU declaration in invoice applies to products 'except where otherwise clearly indicated' – and items are clearly indicated with their specific origins (Germany, USA).
comparison_steps.result: No conflict. Certificate covers both Germany and USA. Invoice confirms both. The EU declaration is supplementary and does not introduce any additional non-covered origin.

rules_applied.description: Origin identification – Germany and USA found as explicit 'Country of Origin' per article
Normalization – Germany and USA match exactly
Mixed origins coupling – satisfied via invoice reference

country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate states origin as 'Germany / USA' in box 3. Invoice contains explicit 'Country of Origin: Germany' for item FT1517 and 'Country of Origin: USA' for item IP0601. Both countries from the certificate can be explicitly identified in the invoice as dedicated origin fields. The coupling of goods to origins is established via the invoice (per rule 4.3.7, since the certificate references the invoice). The invoice also contains a general EU preferential origin declaration, but this applies 'except where otherwise clearly indicated' – and origins ARE clearly indicated per article. This does not create any contradiction. All certificate origin countries are verified in the invoice.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The EU declaration in invoice could conflict with certificate stating 'Germany / USA' since USA is not EU
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: The EU declaration explicitly states 'except where otherwise clearly indicated'. Item IP0601 is clearly indicated as 'Country of Origin: USA'. The declaration thus does not apply to that item. No conflict exists.

country_of_origin.motivation: Certifikatet anger 'Germany / USA' som ursprungsländer. Fakturan anger explicit 'Country of Origin: Germany' för artikel FT1517 och 'Country of Origin: USA' för artikel IP0601. Båda ursprungsländerna kan identifieras i fakturan. MATCH enligt 4.5.1 och 4.5.4.

evidence_records.raw_text: Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A, Barbaros Mah. Kardelen Sok. Palladium Tower No. 2/1 K:37-38, 347 46 Atasehir - Istanbul, Turkey
Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S, Barbaros Mah. Kardelen Sok. Palladium Tower No:2/1 K:37-38, 34746 Ataşehir-İstanbul, Turkey
Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S, Akcansa Çanakkale Plant, Mahmudiye Beldesi, 17640 EZİNE ÇANAKKALE, Turkey
Spare and Wareparts for SNCR-Equipment accoring to Commercial Invoice no. 1418

rule_evaluation_log.description: LC-undantag – check for Letter of Credit reference
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: No LC reference found in certificate. Certificate references are 'PO: 4337 SAS: 4500335660' and 'Inovice no. 1418'. No bankterms context for LC.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Document pairing – certificate file contains COO on page 1 and invoice pages 2-3. Invoice file contains the same invoice on pages 1-2.
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: Certificate extracted from Document A page 1. Invoice for verification extracted from Document B (separate invoice file) pages 1-2. Invoice in certificate file pages 2-3 is embedded supplementary material per rule 8.5.0.
VALID. Three criteria met: (1) invoice reference 1418 matches, (2) consignor NOx-SOL AB matches, (3) consignee Akçansa matches.
MATCH. 'NOx-SOL AB' found in invoice header, signature line, and footer.
MATCH. Sweden confirmed via explicit address and VAT prefix SE.
MATCH. Certificate 'Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A' is exact prefix of invoice 'Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S'. Truncation of '.S' due to field limitation.
MATCH. Invoice no. 1418 and date 2026-02-11 match. Explicit reference present. Invoice content consistent with certificate description.
MATCH. Shipment quantity verified via invoice reference.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Multiple quantities – at least one verified
rule_evaluation_log.outcome: MATCH. Shipment quantity verified. Gross weight 8kg identifiable in invoice.
MATCH. Germany and USA found as explicit Country of Origin per article.
MATCH. Coupling satisfied via invoice reference.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Samtliga kontrollpunkter överensstämmer. Avsändare (NOx-SOL AB, Sweden) och mottagare (Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S, Turkey) kan verifieras mot fakturan. Varubeskrivningen verifieras via hänvisning till faktura nr 1418. Ursprungsländerna Germany och USA framgår explicit i fakturan. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
summary_of_findings: Consignor NOx-SOL AB matches invoice issuer exactly. Country Sweden verified via address and VAT prefix.

Consignee name truncated in certificate ('Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A') – exact prefix of invoice name ('Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S'). Layout truncation rule applied. Country Turkey matches.
Goods description: general description with explicit invoice reference (no. 1418, date 2026-02-11) verified. Invoice content (spare parts for SNCR-equipment) is consistent.
Quantity: shipment quantity (1 package) verified via invoice reference. Gross weight 8kg identifiable in invoice.
Origin: Germany and USA both explicitly stated in invoice per article (Country of Origin fields).

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot faktura
human_readable_report.document_pair: COO SEG-26D-453865 vs Commercial Invoice no. 1418

sections.rule_applied: Koncernstruktur (4.1.0) – consignor namn hittat i fakturans sidhuvud och sidfot. Land verifierat via explicit adress och VAT-prefix SE.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'NOx-SOL AB' kan identifieras exakt i fakturan (sidhuvud, signaturrad, sidfot). Land Sweden bekräftat via adressblock och VAT-nummer (SE-prefix).

sections.certificate_value: Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A, Barbaros Mah. Kardelen Sok. Palladium Tower No. 2/1 K:37-38, 347 46 Atasehir - Istanbul, Turkey
sections.invoice_value: Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S, Barbaros Mah. Kardelen Sok. Palladium Tower No:2/1 K:37-38, 34746 Ataşehir-İstanbul, Turkey
sections.rule_applied: Layoutavkortning (4.2.2.1.1) – certifikatets namn är en exakt prefix av fakturans namn. Avkortningen av '.S' beror på fältbegränsning.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'Akçansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A' är en exakt inledande del av fakturans 'Akcansa Çimento Sanayi ve Ticaret A.S'. Avkortningen av '.S' beror uppenbart på fältbegränsning. Land (Turkey) överensstämmer. Adress överensstämmer.

sections.rule_applied: Generell varubeskrivning med fakturareferens (4.3.5) – certifikatet hänvisar uttryckligen till faktura nr 1418 daterad 2026-02-11. Båda referenserna överensstämmer.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet använder generell varubeskrivning med uttrycklig hänvisning till faktura nr 1418 daterad 2026-02-11. Fakturanummer och datum stämmer exakt. Fakturans varor (reservdelar/komponenter) är förenliga med certifikatets beskrivning.

sections.rule_applied: Försändelsekvantitet (4.4.3.2) – '1 package' verifierad via fakturareferens. Bruttovikt 8 kg identifierbar i faktura.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets försändelsekvantitet (1 paket) verifieras via fakturareferens (4.4.3.2). Bruttovikt 8 kg kan identifieras i fakturans viktangivelse (8kg). Minst en kvantitetsuppgift är verifierad enligt 4.4.3.1.

sections.invoice_value: Country of Origin: Germany (FT1517); Country of Origin: USA (IP0601)
sections.rule_applied: Ursprungsidentifiering (4.5.1) – båda länderna återfinns som explicita 'Country of Origin'-fält i fakturan per artikel.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet anger 'Germany / USA'. Fakturan anger 'Country of Origin: Germany' för FT1517 och 'Country of Origin: USA' för IP0601. Båda ursprungsländerna kan explicit identifieras i fakturan.

output_quality_checks.notes: All 5 checkpoints evaluated. All applicable checkpoints MATCH. Lowest confidence is 0.92 (consignee, quantity) which is above 0.85 threshold. comparison_result IDENTICAL is consistent with all MATCH statuses. No MISMATCH or MANUAL_REVIEW on any checkpoint.
